hidden_disagreement.interpretation: Agents split across recommendations; no hidden-consensus to expose.

low_discrimination_constructs.interpretation: All options score near the middle on this axis (Utilitarian life-saving optimization <-> Fairness through random allocation). Either none of the options addresses this dimension, or agents collectively underweighted it. Worth probing explicitly: does any option strongly hit one pole here?

risk_surface.note: Risks listed here were extracted by pattern matching on agent free responses. They are minority signals worth probing before acting on consensus.

operator_questions: All options scored near the middle on the axis 'Utilitarian life-saving optimization' vs 'Fairness through random allocation'. Does any option deserve a strong position here? If not, is this a dimension being underweighted?
Option C was recommended only by M1 (S). What does this agent see that others missed - or what is it weighing differently?
The contrarian agent recommended E against the majority. Contrarian disagreement is a useful signal: what is the strongest argument against the majority choice that hasn't been answered?
headline: Agents are split across options; no clear consensus to act on.
